nodes.title: Tokenize className before class checks
nodes.why: Splitting on whitespace makes class matching reliable for values like "item big".
nodes.tradeoff: A tiny parsing cost, but no partial-word class bugs.

nodes.title: Return false early for invalid inputs
nodes.why: Early guards keep the matcher predictable and avoid downstream errors.
nodes.alternative: Let invalid values flow into parsing logic.
nodes.tradeoff: Extra guard lines, but simpler behavior.

nodes.title: Handle #id selectors with a dedicated branch
nodes.why: ID selectors are common and straightforward: compare exact id string.
nodes.alternative: Use one generic parser for all selector types.
nodes.tradeoff: More branching, but easier to read and debug.

nodes.title: Handle .class selectors directly
nodes.why: This keeps class-only selectors simple and avoids unnecessary tag parsing.
nodes.tradeoff: Adds a special case, but clearer intent.

nodes.title: Compare tag names case-insensitively
nodes.why: DOM-like inputs may use different casing; lowercasing keeps matches stable.
nodes.tradeoff: One normalization step, but much fewer surprises.

nodes.title: If class exists, enforce both tag and class
nodes.why: For selectors like button.primary, both pieces must match to return true.
nodes.alternative: Treat tag.class as tag OR class.
nodes.tradeoff: Stricter logic, but correct selector semantics.
